scopes.name: default
scopes.paradigm: Class-based HTTP client with parallel sync and async APIs: `httpx.Client()` for sync, `httpx.AsyncClient()` for async — both subclass `BaseClient` and support context managers (`with`/`async with`) enforced by a `ClientState` enum (UNOPENED → OPENED → CLOSED) that prevents reuse after close. All constructor arguments are keyword-only and passed directly to `__init__` (auth, headers, cookies, timeout, limits, base_url, transport, mounts, follow_redirects, event_hooks, trust_env, default_encoding); there is no factory function, no `.create()`, no `.extend()`. The `transport=` parameter accepts a `BaseTransport`/`AsyncBaseTransport` subclass that implements a single method (`handle_request` / `handle_async_request`), making the transport layer swappable without touching the client API — transports are concrete classes, not Protocol or ABC, but use `raise NotImplementedError` as the override contract. A `mounts=` dict maps URL prefix patterns to transports, providing internal per-URL transport routing without any public `.mount()` method. Configuration objects (`Timeout`, `Limits`, `Proxy`) are plain classes with typed fields, not TypedDict or dataclass. Type aliases for flexible inputs (`HeaderTypes`, `AuthTypes`, `TimeoutTypes`, `QueryParamTypes`) are broad `Union` types defined in `_types.py` — the client normalises them internally. Auth is class-based: subclass `Auth`, override the generator method `auth_flow(request) -> Generator[Request, Response, None]`; built-ins include `BasicAuth`, `DigestAuth`, `NetRCAuth`, and `FunctionAuth` wrapping a plain callable — no middleware chain, no interceptor class, no per-request hook objects. Event hooks are lists of plain callables stored under `event_hooks={'request': [...], 'response': [...]}` — called imperatively during the send cycle, not via EventEmitter. Streaming responses require `client.stream(...)` as a context manager (`@contextmanager` / `@asynccontextmanager`). The exception hierarchy is deep and documented: `HTTPError` → `RequestError` → `TransportError` → `TimeoutException` (ConnectTimeout, ReadTimeout, WriteTimeout, PoolTimeout), `NetworkError` (ConnectError, ReadError, WriteError, CloseError), `ProtocolError` (LocalProtocolError, RemoteProtocolError), `ProxyError`, `UnsupportedProtocol`; and separately `HTTPStatusError` (raised by `.raise_for_status()`), `InvalidURL`, `CookieConflict`, and `StreamError` subclasses for misuse of the streaming API. Conspicuously absent: no requests-style `Session.mount()` public method, no urllib3-style connection pool objects surfaced to callers, no aiohttp-style `connector=` kwarg, no callbacks or Promises, no `asyncio.create_task`-style fire-and-forget, no subclassing of Client as the intended extension point (transport injection is preferred), no `requests.adapters.HTTPAdapter`, no `__all__` re-exporting transport internals.
